task_completion.description: The 'Domestic' shipping zone settings page is accessed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WooCommerce Settings > Shipping tab is active, and the 'Domestic' zone is selected or its specific settings page is open.

task_completion.description: A 'Flat rate' shipping method is added to the 'Domestic' zone.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A shipping method of type 'Flat rate' is visible in the shipping methods list for the 'Domestic' zone, or the 'Add shipping method' modal is open with 'Flat rate' selected.

task_completion.description: The shipping method title is set to 'Volume Shipping'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Method title' input field in the Flat rate settings modal contains 'Volume Shipping', or it is listed as 'Volume Shipping' in the shipping methods list.

task_completion.description: The cost calculation formula is set to '10.00 + (2.50 * [qty])'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Cost' input field in the Flat rate settings modal contains the formula '10.00 + (2.50 * [qty])'. Spacing may vary slightly, but the numbers, operators, and '[qty]' placeholder must be present.
In the shipping methods list for the 'Domestic' zone, the toggle switch under the 'Enabled' column for the 'Volume Shipping' method is turned on (typically indicated by a colored toggle).

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress/WooCommerce GUI to configure the shipping rate.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the WordPress admin dashboard and WooCommerce settings pages being used, rather than terminal windows or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual web application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser address bar shows a WordPress admin URL and the standard WordPress interface is visible, not a text editor or mock page.

integrity.description: The agent did not use direct database manipulation to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: No database management tools like phpMyAdmin or MySQL command line interfaces are visible in the screenshots.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target Zone: 'Domestic'. Expected Method Title: 'Volume Shipping'. Expected Cost Formula: '10.00 + (2.50 * [qty])'. The formula uses the standard WooCommerce [qty] placeholder. The VLM should verify that the formula is entered correctly (ignoring spaces, e.g., '10.00+(2.50*[qty])' is acceptable).
